steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The blank is the subject noun phrase, consisting of a title (“German biologist”), a name (“Gertrud Morlock”), and an additional noun (“colleagues”) to be conjoined with “and”.

steps.title: Apply the rule
steps.content:
A restrictive appositive—essential information identifying a noun—is not set off by commas. “German biologist” directly identifies Gertrud Morlock, so no comma separates them; the subject is “[German biologist Gertrud Morlock] and [colleagues]” with no comma before “and”.

The others incorrectly insert commas before the name (C), before the conjunction (B), or at the subject's end (A).

That matches choice D — D is correct.
